rows.notes: Clear centered year at top of page, no monetary amounts, perfectly legible.
Title below year; handwriting legible though the first word is slightly stylised ('Dons' / 'Dons'/'Doms' shape). No amounts in this label.

Right-hand cluster shows a three-digit pounds figure (read as 543) and rightmost cluster clearly reads '9½' (interpreted as pence 9 with 0.5 fraction). The middle (shillings) area is ambiguous because '43' appears close to the pounds region; chose shillings=9 based on column alignment of the '9' immediately left of the pence '9½'. Moderate uncertainty due to crowded digits near the pounds/shillings boundary.

Left-most group interpreted as pounds = 237. Right-most shows '9½' pence again. A small '3' sits in the middle column area which I read as shillings=3. Column boundaries are slightly uncertain here (digits crowd), so confidence reduced.

Short amount at right reads '7 3 4' across the columns; each part aligns reasonably with pounds/shillings/pence zones. Handwriting of description partially abbreviated ('Divd'), but numeric alignment is moderately clear.

This line contains a sub‑item with its amount written inline in the description (9.16.0) rather than in the right-hand £ s d columns. No separate main-column amount visible; thus left blank for the standard columns.
Second sub‑payment of the Marston composition series appears in description (8.12.0). No amount in the main £ s d columns; treated as a descriptive sub-item.
Third sub‑payment given inline as 12.5.0. Again appears inside description text; no separate main-column amount.

There is an underlined figure '30:13:0' directly beneath the Marston composition lines. That value is placed near the centre/description area but clearly represents a subtotal; I placed it into the main columns as pounds=30, shillings=13, pence=0. Moderate confidence because the number sits in the description zone rather than exactly in the right-hand columns.
Right-hand value reads clearly as '13 1 0' aligned with the £ s d columns. Description is legible as 'Late Rentors Prize'.
Numerals '17 10 0' are clearly written and aligned in the £ s d columns. Description 'Dr. Jones legacy' is legible; reading confident.

Amount reads as '2 2 0' in the columns. The description appears to read '1/4 to K... Warming Hall' — the first word is unclear (initial looks like 'K' or 'Kinar'), so I flagged moderate confidence for the description but the numeric reading is clear.
Right column shows '3 0 0' aligned across £ s d. Description reads as '3 Sets of rooms. Tutor' (handwriting legible).
Amount '43 0 0' is clearly aligned and legible. The name/word 'Dickerson' is readable in context as the payer; high confidence.

rows.description: Room Rent except y. or moiety
rows.notes: Amount appears as '122 0 0' in the £ s d columns. Description is partly abbreviated ('except y. or moity') but the numeric value is clear.

Right-hand reading looks like '6 11 8½' (eight and a half pence). The fraction mark resembles the loop for a half‑penny; columns align reasonably. Moderate confidence because the fractional mark is small and the handwriting slightly cramped.
Amount '5 0 0' is clear and well aligned. Description reads 'Hutt for stone' (handwriting legible).
This line shows a clear amount '552 12 4' in the £ s d columns and the description 'Sale of Consols & In[come] net' is legible. Very high confidence.
Large, clear entry '8000 0 0' aligned in the pounds column; description ('Sale of Estate & Pen') readable. No ambiguity.
Amount '65 0 0' is neatly written in the £ s d columns. Description is legible; very high confidence.

This row is somewhat compressed. The left part of the description reads '283.0.11 Red 3 per cents' but the right-column numeric appears as '256 5 8' (I transcribed the right-hand number as the ledger credit). Moderate confidence owing to the proximity of two different numeric groups (one in the description, one in the columns) and some smudging.
Final underlined total at bottom right reads clearly as '10075 4 9½' (pounds 10075, shillings 4, pence 9 and a half). Underline and alignment make this value very legible.
